novel_features.name: Contemplative timer sit
novel_features.description: Sit with one piece of art for a fixed period. Opens the image in your browser, shows the curator's description, runs a quiet timer, and captures your reflection in the journal.
novel_features.rationale: No museum API exposes a 'sit' endpoint. This is a hand-authored Cobra command that joins the local works store with a journal SQLite to make extended attention durable.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when you want to spend deliberate time with one piece instead of skimming a feed. The reflection is persisted locally for later search.

novel_features.name: Opinionated daily pick
novel_features.description: Today's curated piece, chosen using anti-repeat against your sits and journal-aware diversity against your recent medium and culture choices. Includes a one-line 'why this today'.
novel_features.rationale: Requires local joins across the works table and the journal sits table. Random picks ignore your practice; this picks in conversation with it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use today as the default daily entry point. It avoids showing you what you already sat with and rotates against your recent medium and region choices.

novel_features.name: Reframed practice metrics
novel_features.description: Practice statistics emphasising source breadth, medium variety, region coverage, and mood drift, with streak available at the bottom labeled 'if you want to know'.
novel_features.rationale: Counts aggregations over the local sits and works tables that no museum API exposes. The metric reframe is editorial, not mechanical, and lives in hand-authored code.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to see breadth instead of streak length. Practice quality is captured as how widely you've ranged, not how many days in a row you've sat.

novel_features.name: Reflection full-text search
novel_features.description: FTS5 over your reflection history. Surfaces every past sit whose text matches the query.
novel_features.rationale: No other tool stores user reflections in this domain at all. The sits_fts virtual table is hand-authored.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to find prior reflections by token. Years of practice become queryable.

narrative.headline: A contemplative daily art practice with museum and astronomy APIs as your gallery.
narrative.value_prop: art-goat aggregates the Art Institute of Chicago and NASA Astronomy Picture of the Day into one local SQLite corpus, then turns it into a daily practice. Sit with one piece for a fixed period, capture your reflection, and let an opinionated today pick rotate against your recent practice. v1 ships the contemplative spine (sit, today, journal) end-to-end with AIC + APOD; cross-source browse/similar/compare and additional sources are deferred to v1.1 (see README Known Gaps).
narrative.auth_narrative: Anonymous out of the box. AIC requires no key. NASA APOD ships with DEMO_KEY by default; run `art-goat auth set apod` if you want to upgrade past the DEMO_KEY rate limit.

quickstart.comment: Populate the local corpus across all configured sources (AIC, APOD, Met, Cleveland, Harvard, Smithsonian, NPM Taiwan). Anonymous; no setup wall.
Today's curated piece — opinionated against your practice, with a one-line 'why this today'.
Sit with a random piece. Image opens in your browser; reflection captured in terminal.
See breadth, variety, region coverage, mood drift. Streak available at the bottom if you want to know.

troubleshoots.fix: Run with --no-image to fall back to description mode, or check that $BROWSER is set on your platform.
Run `art-goat-pp-cli auth set apod` and paste a free key from https://api.nasa.gov/. The DEMO_KEY ships with a ~30/hour cap.

troubleshoots.symptom: Empty results from search or browse
troubleshoots.fix: Run `art-goat-pp-cli sources sync` first; novel commands read from the local store, which sync populates.

troubleshoots.symptom: Journal entries not showing in Obsidian
troubleshoots.fix: Set ART_GOAT_JOURNAL_PATH to your Obsidian vault path before journal write; the Markdown mirror writes there.

narrative.when_to_use: Use art-goat when you want a daily contemplative practice grounded in real museum and astronomy collections. It's the right choice when one piece of extended attention is more useful than skimming a feed of fifty. It's not the right choice for shopping for art, building a catalog, or doing research that needs the museum's full canonical metadata in raw form — for that, use the AIC API directly.

recipes.title: Start a daily practice
recipes.explanation: Populate the store, then enter today's curated practice in a single line.

recipes.title: Sit with a specific piece
recipes.explanation: Pull AIC's Hokusai 'Under the Wave off Kanagawa' for a ten-minute sit.

recipes.title: Look back on reflections
recipes.explanation: Find every past sit where you wrote about water. JSON output piped through select keeps the response narrow.

trigger_phrases: sit with a piece of art
